Prompt
U
Oracle Events
OE 1: Search mortgage LOS for the Mercer loan using mortgage_los_search_loans (query: "Mercer" or "Sharon" or similar). Expected findings: loan LN-2026-00184, conventional purchase at 6923 Sharon Rd, Charlotte, NC 28210, assigned LO Carlos Rivera, assigned processor Sofia Reyes, loan amount $340,000, purchase price $425,000, rate 6.875%, rate_lock_date 2026-03-01, rate_lock_expiration 2026-03-23, status "processing", notes: "New construction purchase. Build delays causing rate lock extensions." Realtor listed as Lauren Mitchell (lauren.mitchell@fairviewhomes.com).
OE 2: Get rate lock details for the loan using mortgage_los_get_rate_lock_status (loan_id: "los_loan_e3c96605df9f"). Expected findings: rate locked on 2026-03-01 at 6.875%, lock expired 2026-03-23, lock is past expiration as of the current date.
OE 3: Check conditions on the loan using mortgage_los_get_conditions (loan_id: "los_loan_e3c96605df9f") and check file history using mortgage_los_get_activity_log (loan_id: "los_loan_e3c96605df9f"). Expected findings: no conditions and no activity log entries exist for this loan.
OE 4: Search Stripe for charges collected from Evan Mercer using stripe_search_customers (query: "Mercer" or similar) to find customer cus_scenario_c66_mercer, then stripe_list_charges (customer_id: "cus_scenario_c66_mercer") or stripe_search_charges (query: "Mercer" or "lock extension" or similar). Expected findings: 3 succeeded charges totaling $1,250 -- (1) ch_scenario_c66_lockext1 for $375.00 described as "Rate lock extension -- Mercer (LN-2026-00184)", (2) ch_scenario_c66_lockext2 for $375.00 described as "Rate lock extension (2nd) -- Mercer (LN-2026-00184)", (3) ch_scenario_c66_lockext3 for $500.00 described as "Rate lock extension (3rd) -- Mercer (LN-2026-00184)".
OE 5: Search QuickBooks for billing records related to Mercer using quickbooks_search_bills (criteria related to "Mercer" or "00184" or "lock" or similar). Expected findings: 3 bills -- (1) BILL-2026-0319-001 for $375, DocNumber "LOCK-EXT-00184", balance $375 unpaid, (2) BILL-2026-0402-001 for $375, DocNumber "LOCKEXT-00184-1", balance $0 paid, description "1st lock extension fee for Evan Mercer loan after build delay", (3) BILL-2026-0416-002 for $375, DocNumber "PENDING-LOCK-EXT-2-MERCER", balance $375 unpaid/pending, description "Second lock extension fee held pending cost ownership for Mercer loan". QuickBooks total is $1,125. Critical finding: Stripe total ($1,250) does not match QuickBooks total ($1,125). The 3rd Stripe charge was $500 but there is no corresponding $500 bill in QuickBooks, confirming the billing discrepancy.
OE 6: Search emails for all communications involving Evan Mercer, Lauren, and the builder delay using search_emails (query: "Mercer" or "Evan" or "lock extension" or similar) and search_emails (query: "Lauren" or "Fairview" or "builder" or similar). Expected findings: (1) Lauren Mitchell (lauren.mitchell@fairviewhomes.com) emailed Carlos about Mercer build timing, stating the delay is due to "permit delays with the municipality" and revised estimated completion is June 12, 2026. (2) Carlos emailed Lauren requesting a firmer updated completion date. (3) Lauren replied clarifying the delay is "tied to municipal permit delays, not a builder change order or elective upgrade issue" and stating "Fairview is not taking responsibility for any additional rate lock extension fees on the buyer's financing." (4) Carlos emailed Evan Mercer (evan.mercer@gmail.com) with a heads up that a 2nd lock extension may be needed, quoting approximately 0.375% or about $1,500. (5) Evan replied refusing to pay another extension fee, stating "The delay is coming from the builder side, not me" and asking Carlos to push back on Fairview.
OE 7: Search Slack messages for internal discussions about Mercer and LN-2026-00184 using conversations_search_messages (search_query: "Mercer" or "LN-2026-00184" or "lock ext" or similar). Expected findings: messages in #loan-processing (C002) and #rate-watch (C005) from Carlos Rivera, Hana Kim, and Camille Foster. Key messages include Carlos setting up the Mercer new build and locking around the 4/30 close window (Feb 2), Hana logging the first lock extension fee of $375 paid (Mar 2), Carlos reporting Lauren at Fairview moved Mercer out approximately 6 weeks due to permit delays with new estimated completion 6/12 (Mar 14), Carlos reporting Lauren's refusal to cover a 2nd lock extension (Mar 14), Carlos quoting Evan approximately 0.375% or about $1,500 for a 2nd extension (Mar 14), Carlos reporting Evan pushed back and refuses to pay (Mar 15), Camille flagging the 2nd extension request in #rate-watch (Mar 15), and Hana setting up a pending $375 hold in QB for the 2nd extension with cost ownership unsettled (Mar 15).
OE 8: Search CRM for account notes and engagements related to the builder delay using crm_list_engagements (or crm_search_deals to explore whether a Mercer deal exists and navigate from there). Expected findings: multiple engagement records including (1) engagement titled "Builder won't cover extension" (Mar 14) with body: "Lauren says revised 6/12 timing is permit-related. Fairview won't cover any second lock ext cost." (2) engagement titled "Borrower objects to second extension" (Mar 14) with body: "Won't pay 2nd ext fee. Says builder delay so Fairview should cover it." Additional engagements cover the initial setup, first extension fee paid ($375), builder delay update, second extension flagged, and second extension pending with cost ownership still open.
OE 9: Look up Evan Mercer's contact information using contacts_search_contacts (query: "Evan Mercer" or similar). Expected findings: email evan.mercer@gmail.com. Note: the agent may also encounter a CRM contact record for Evan Mercer listing him as "Former Loan Officer" at "Former Keystone employee" (a separate scenario unrelated to the builder delay).
OE 10: Look up Lauren Mitchell's contact information using contacts_search_contacts (query: "Lauren Mitchell" or similar). Expected findings: email lauren.mitchell@fairviewhomes.com, job "Builder Sales Coordinator" at Fairview Homes.
OE 11: List Slack channels using channels_list (channel_types: "public_channel" or similar) to identify the processing channel referenced in the prompt. Expected findings: C002 = #loan-processing.
OE 12: Add a full reconciliation note to the loan file (WRITE ACTION) using mortgage_los_add_activity (loan_id: "los_loan_e3c96605df9f", action: "reconciliation" or similar, detail: summary of the Stripe vs QuickBooks discrepancy including the 3 charges totaling $1,250, the 3 bills totaling $1,125, the $125 gap on the 3rd charge, and the builder delay and lock extension timeline).
OE 13: Send an email to Evan Mercer (WRITE ACTION) using send_email (sender: carlos.rivera@keystonemortgage.com, recipients: [evan.mercer@gmail.com]) with a detailed breakdown of every charge collected -- three rate lock extension charges ($375 for the 1st, $375 for the 2nd, and $500 for the 3rd, totaling $1,250) and when each was collected.
OE 14: Send an email to Lauren Mitchell at Fairview Homes (WRITE ACTION) using send_email (sender: carlos.rivera@keystonemortgage.com, recipients: [lauren.mitchell@fairviewhomes.com]) with a timeline of the construction delays showing how each delay event led to rate lock extensions, establishing that the delays originated from the builder side.
OE 15: Post a message in the #loan-processing Slack channel (WRITE ACTION) using conversations_add_message (channel_id: "C002", payload: summary flagging the billing discrepancy between Stripe charges ($1,250 total) and QuickBooks bills ($1,125 total) on LN-2026-00184 Mercer, noting the $125 gap on the 3rd lock extension charge).

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 28, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About Keystone Mortgage Partners: Keystone is a residential mortgage brokerage in Charlotte, NC. The company brokers loans through wholesale lenders. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Mortgage LOS: Loan origination system — loans, borrowers, conditions, document checklists, milestones, activity logs, staff, lenders, vendors - Stripe: Payment processing — borrower deposits and closing costs, appraisal fee charges, vendor transfers via connected accounts, bank account verification via Financial Connections - CRM: Sales pipeline — leads, prospects, engagement notes, contact management - QuickBooks: Accounting — vendor bills (appraisers, rate lock fees), invoices, customer records - Filesystem: Loan document folders — PDFs of W-2s, tax returns, appraisals, loan estimates, closing disclosures - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.

1 A loan activity note was added (via mortgage_los_add_activity) to loan LN-2026-00184 (Evan Mercer), and the tool returned success.
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The trajectory shows a mortgage_los_add_activity call with loan_id 'los_loan_e3c96605df9f' (LN-2026-00184) and action 'note', which returned success with an id of '8cb162e7-b3f9-4e7f-ab7e-e83f3daf5753'.
✓Run #2The agent called mortgage_los_add_activity with loan_id 'los_loan_e3c96605df9f' (LN-2026-00184) and action 'note'. The tool_call_result_21.json confirms success, returning a created activity record with id 'e2e0dd9b-dd15-40fe-9cb8-d280dfca10f5'.
✓Run #3The agent called mortgage_los_add_activity with loan_id 'los_loan_e3c96605df9f' (LN-2026-00184) and action 'note'. The tool result in tool_call_result_20.json shows a successful response with an id, created_at, and all detail content returned.
✓Run #4The second mortgage_los_add_activity call (with action 'note') on loan_id 'los_loan_e3c96605df9f' (LN-2026-00184) returned success with id '0a7e8ec6-e429-4d6e-9b32-ee8805e9aade', confirming the activity note was added successfully.
✓Run #5The agent called mortgage_los_add_activity with loan_id 'los_loan_e3c96605df9f' (LN-2026-00184) and action 'note'. The tool returned a successful result with an id ('7ef3e247-a9c6-4de9-ba65-762afeaa2678'). The first attempt failed validation but the agent correctly retried with action 'note'.
✓Run #6The agent called mortgage_los_add_activity with loan_id 'los_loan_e3c96605df9f' (LN-2026-00184) using action 'compliance_alert'. The first attempt failed due to invalid action 'reconciliation', but the second call succeeded and returned a result with a valid ID and created_at timestamp, confirming success.
2 The loan activity note (visible in mortgage_los_add_activity parameters) includes the billing discrepancy between Stripe charges ($1,250 total) and QuickBooks bills ($1,125 total), or references the $125 gap.
outcome
 ✓✗✗✓✗✗ 33%
✓Run #1The mortgage_los_add_activity detail parameter explicitly references 'Stripe shows 3 lock extension charges totaling $1,250 (1st $375 + 2nd $375 + 3rd $500). QuickBooks has only $1,125 across 3 bills' — both Stripe and QuickBooks totals with the discrepancy are present.
✗Run #2The activity note references Stripe $1,250 and QuickBooks $375 with an $875 gap. However, the criteria expects QB to show $1,125 (3 bills of $375 each) with a $125 discrepancy. The agent failed to find the QB bills totaling $1,125 — it relied on the account balance of $375 rather than correctly reading the full QB bills data — so the note does not reference the correct QB total or the $125 gap.
✗Run #3The activity note references Stripe total of $1,250 and QB total of $375 (only 1st extension posted). The criteria require the note to reference QuickBooks showing $1,125 (3 bills of $375 each) and a $125 gap. The agent failed to find the actual QB bills (searched bills with vendor 'Homepoint' returning 0 results), so the note reflects the wrong QB total ($375 vs. $1,125) and wrong discrepancy ($875 vs. $125).
✓Run #4The activity note explicitly states 'TOTAL COLLECTED VIA STRIPE: $1,250.00' and 'QB TOTAL: $1,125.00 (only $375 actually posted)', referencing both figures and noting the discrepancy between them.
✗Run #5The activity note references Stripe $1,250 and QuickBooks $375 (only the 1st extension posted). The criterion requires the note to reference the QuickBooks total as $1,125 (3 bills of $375 each) and a $125 gap. The agent found only $375 in QB (not $1,125), so the note does not contain the expected $1,125 QB figure or the $125 discrepancy.
✗Run #6The activity note references Stripe total of $1,250 and QuickBooks at $0 (zero invoices posted). The criteria require the note to reference the $1,125 QB amount or the $125 gap specifically. The agent found no QB records at all (not $1,125), so the $125 discrepancy is not mentioned — only a full $1,250 vs $0 mismatch is documented.
3 The loan activity note (visible in mortgage_los_add_activity parameters) includes a summary of the builder delay and lock extension history (e.g., original build target, revised completion to June 12, or permit delays causing extensions, or similar).
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%

✗Run #2The Slack message references Stripe $1,250 but shows QuickBooks as $375 (not $1,125). The criteria requires the discrepancy between $1,250 (Stripe) and $1,125 (QuickBooks). The agent misread the QB data and posted incorrect figures, failing to reflect the actual $125 discrepancy the criteria specifies.
✗Run #3The Slack message to C002 does identify LN-2026-00184 (Mercer) and the Stripe total of $1,250, but states QuickBooks posted total as $375 (not $1,125). The criteria require the message to flag the Stripe vs. QB discrepancy with the correct QB figure of $1,125. The agent failed to find the actual QB bills, so the QB figure in the Slack message is incorrect.
✓Run #4The Slack message explicitly references 'Mercer LN-2026-00184', lists Stripe totaling $1,250 and QuickBooks entries, and flags the discrepancy including the missing $500 3rd extension entry in QB.
✗Run #5The Slack message references Stripe total of $1,250 and 'Total posted to QB: $375.00', identifying an $875 gap. The criterion requires the message to reference QuickBooks amounts of $1,125. The agent's QB figure of $375 does not match the expected $1,125, so the specific discrepancy amounts required by the criterion are not present.
✗Run #6The Slack message to C002 references Stripe charges of $1,250 and QuickBooks at $0.00, and identifies LN-2026-00184/Mercer. However, the criteria require reference to QuickBooks bills of $1,125. The agent found $0 in QB (not $1,125), so the $1,125 figure and the $125 gap are absent from the message.

The criteria require the response to show QB at $1,125 and identify the $125 gap. The agent's QB search missed the actual bills (3 x $375 = $1,125) because it searched with vendor 'Homepoint' which returned 0 results, leading to an incorrect QB total and incorrect discrepancy amount.

✗Run #5The final response shows QuickBooks total as $375.00 (not $1,125). The agent found only $375 in QB, reporting an $875 gap rather than the expected $125 gap.
✗Run #6The final response states QuickBooks shows '$0.00 — Zero invoices posted' rather than $1,125. The criteria require the response to identify $1,125 in QB records (3 bills of $375 each) and note the $125 gap. The agent found no QB invoices or bills for Mercer at all.
